mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in advance retail sales indicates strengthening consumer expenditure that translates into higher corporate revenues and positive earnings surprises, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS do not correlate with positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.25% represents sufficiently restrictive monetary policy that elevates the real cost of capital and triggers de-risking in equity markets, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate above 4% and a decline in nonfarm payrolls confirms a labor market contraction that reduces aggregate income and consumer demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of UNRATE > 4% and falling PAYEMS fails to predict negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of instances, or the interaction term is insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions where restrictive monetary policy has elevated borrowing costs, a month-over-month decline in industrial production signals that supply constraints are binding and corporate profit margins are compressing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion under restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: During identified late-cycle periods, month-over-month declines in INDPRO are not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that supports near-term consumer confidence and spending expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in UNRATE are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer prices accelerate while the federal funds rate remains above 5%, the real policy rate is insufficiently restrictive to curb inflation, eroding corporate pricing power and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS > 5% does not produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth captures the economy's underlying demand trajectory; positive quarter-over-quarter growth in chained-dollar output confirms expanding economic activity that supports corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth does not correlate with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns (Rank IC < 0.02 over a 5-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1,300 thousand units signal a cooling residential construction sector that reduces demand for building materials, consumer durables, and mortgage credit, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST levels below 1,300 thousand are not followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances.
